idea: Terraform-managed VPC and private build host with SSM access
why_this_project_matters: This project adds networking, IAM, and compute in a way that encourages secure administration without opening SSH to the internet.
architecture: Terraform provisions a VPC, a private subnet, a security group with tightly scoped egress, and an EC2 instance managed through AWS Systems Manager instead of public SSH exposure. IAM attaches only the SSM-managed instance core policy needed for remote access.
steps: Initialize the Terraform project and provide your target VPC CIDR and AMI values.
Apply the network and compute resources in a non-production account first.
Connect to the instance through AWS Systems Manager Session Manager instead of SSH.
Install build tooling or runners after the base host is reachable.
Replace the single host with an autoscaled or managed service when usage grows.
